title: T-093 — API: NPD writes draft BOM into bom_headers/bom_lines + back-fill migration
prompt:
Switch NPD from the legacy computed BOM view to writing real `bom_headers/bom_lines` rows (status='draft', origin_module='npd') at FA creation, and back-fill existing FA records.

Deliverables:
1. Server Action `createDraftBomForFa(faId)` in `apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/_actions/create-draft-bom.ts`:
   - Inserts `bom_headers` row (status='draft', origin_module='npd', fa_id, version='0.1.0').
   - Inserts `bom_lines` rows by reading the FA's `prod_detail` components (the same dataset the legacy computed view used).
   - Idempotent: if an active draft exists for fa_id, returns existing header_id without insert.
   - Wrapped in withOrgContext + outbox emits `bom.draft_created`.
2. Hook the action into the existing FA-creation Server Action (called immediately after FA insert).
3. Update existing NPD readers (e.g. `getFaBom(faId)`) to query `bom_headers` directly via fa_id (or product_id once promoted), removing reads from the legacy computed view.
4. Promotion helper `promoteBomToProduct(faId, productId)` — when an FA graduates to FG, sets `bom_headers.product_id` (keeps fa_id for traceability) and transitions status as appropriate (draft remains draft until Technical approves).
5. Back-fill migration `0093_bom_npd_backfill.sql` — for every FA with no existing draft bom_header, insert one via SQL using prod_detail rows. Wrap in idempotent INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING using the partial index from T-092.
6. After back-fill verified, DROP the legacy NPD computed view in the same migration (with comment explaining replacement) — only AFTER all FA rows have a header.

RED tests:
- Unit: createDraftBomForFa idempotency (calling twice yields one header).
- Unit: prod_detail with N components → N bom_lines rows with correct qty/uom.
- Integration: getFaBom returns rows from bom_headers (no view dependency); fail if it still hits the legacy view (grep guard).
- Migration test: dry-run on a fixture with 5 FAs, 3 already with manual bom_headers → back-fill produces exactly 2 new headers.

Do NOT alter bom_lines schema. Do NOT change Technical's approval flow. Do NOT promote draft → approved automatically (Technical owns that transition).

Close-out: paste vitest output + migration filename + grep guard output confirming legacy view dropped + outbox event names.

pipeline_inputs.description: NPD creates draft bom_headers/lines on FA creation; back-fill existing FAs; drop legacy view; switch readers.
pipeline_inputs.details: Idempotent Server Action wired into FA creation; promotion helper sets product_id when FA → FG; back-fill migration covers existing data BEFORE the view is dropped; readers retargeted to bom_headers; outbox emits bom.draft_created.
scope_files: packages/domain/src/npd/bom/get-fa-bom.ts [edit — switch from view to bom_headers]
acceptance_criteria: Given an FA is created with 3 components in prod_detail, when create-fa action commits, then exactly 1 bom_headers row (status='draft', origin_module='npd', fa_id=...) and 3 bom_lines rows exist.
Given createDraftBomForFa is called twice for the same FA, when committed, then only 1 bom_headers row exists (idempotency).
Given an FA graduates to FG, when promoteBomToProduct(faId, productId) runs, then bom_headers.product_id is set and fa_id remains.
Given the back-fill migration runs against 5 FAs (3 already with manual headers), when committed, then exactly 2 new headers exist.
Given a grep of the codebase, when run, then no reader references the legacy computed NPD BOM view.
Given the legacy NPD computed view, when migration completes, then the view is DROPPED.
Given `pnpm vitest run` for new tests, then 0 failures.
test_strategy: RED unit tests for idempotency and N-components → N-lines.
RED integration test for getFaBom returning header-driven rows.
RED migration dry-run test for 5-FA fixture.
Grep guard for legacy view name.
risk_red_lines: Do not drop the legacy view BEFORE back-fill is verified — same migration must do back-fill first then DROP VIEW.
Do not auto-promote draft → approved (Technical's responsibility).
Do not bypass withOrgContext or skip outbox.
Do not duplicate BOM lines on re-runs.
